observations: Ground-mounted commercial or large residential solar array visible — multiple rows of panels installed on open grassland.
Customer pitch phase: worker is using a door-knock or on-site conversation; transcript captured two key statements.
No worker visible in frame — likely showing the customer a reference install site or project photo as part of the pitch.

salesPitchFeedback.message: CRITIQUE — 'Everyone on this street is getting it' is a high-pressure fear-of-missing-out tactic. Under Australian Consumer Law this can be considered misleading if not accurate, and it erodes trust fast. Customers Google you after you leave.

CRITIQUE — 'Don't worry about the details, just sign here today' is a red flag for any customer. It signals the worker is hiding something and will likely kill the sale or trigger a cooling-off cancellation. Never rush a signature by dismissing concerns.

STRONGER LINE — Replace the FOMO line with: 'A few of your neighbours have already locked in their systems and are seeing bills drop by around 70%. I can show you exactly what that would look like for your home.'

STRONGER LINE — Replace 'don't worry about details' with: 'Let me walk you through the key points — the system size, payback period, and what the warranty covers — so you feel completely comfortable before we proceed.'

RAPPORT TIP — Lead with a personalised savings figure. Ask for a recent power bill first, calculate their payback on the spot (aim for under 5 years), then anchor the conversation on ROI, not urgency.
CLOSE TIP — Use a soft close: 'If everything stacks up for you today, is there anything stopping us from getting the install date locked in?' This invites objections rather than steamrolling them.

WARRANTY TIP — Proactively mention the panel product warranty (typically 25 years) and workmanship warranty. Customers who understand the warranty are far less likely to cancel during the cooling-off period.

timeOnTaskNote: Pitch phase — if the worker is showing this array as a reference site, that is good visual proof. Ensure the proof-point conversation does not exceed 2–3 minutes before pivoting back to the customer's specific savings and next steps.
nextSteps: Ask the customer for their latest electricity bill and calculate their estimated payback period on the spot — anchor the pitch in their real numbers.
Walk through the three key facts every customer needs before signing: system size and output, payback period, and what the warranties cover.
Use a soft trial close: 'Does the payback period make sense for you?' — surface objections early rather than pushing for a rushed signature.

visualCallouts.label: Show this array
visualCallouts.message: Point to this array and say: this is the scale we can achieve — your rooftop system will generate similar clean energy and slash your bill.

visualCallouts.label: Upsell: battery storage
visualCallouts.message: A ground or roof array this size pairs perfectly with a battery — mention battery storage to capture excess generation and quote accordingly.

spokenCue.say: Drop the pressure tactics — ask for their bill and run the numbers.
